title: China-to-Tunisia Solar PV Inverter Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.target_requirement: Tunisia's electricity system uses 50 Hz, and STEG grid settings for PV connection should account for the cited 47.5-52 Hz frequency window and nominal-voltage tolerance of -15% / +10%. PV inverters for Tunisia should be configured and documented for the local voltage level, phase arrangement, protection thresholds, frequency ride-through or disconnection behavior, and STEG project settings.
target_standards: Tunisia 50 Hz public electricity system
STEG cited grid operating range: 47.5-52 Hz and nominal voltage -15% / +10%
STEG project-specific grid-connection settings
Applicable low-voltage or medium-voltage connection procedure
rows.china_equivalent: China also uses 50 Hz, so nominal frequency is usually not the main gap. The gap is the allowed voltage and frequency windows, protection curves, anti-islanding trip settings, reactive power functions, export limitation logic, and the documented settings required by the Tunisian project.
rows.gap: Ship with a Tunisia-specific grid profile or a controlled commissioning process. Record firmware version, country code, protection settings, password control, and any field changes made by the installer or STEG-approved commissioning team.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory as a technical compatibility and connection-approval matter for grid-connected installations; exact settings are project and STEG-review dependent
mandatory_status.cn: China 50 Hz compatibility is relevant but not sufficient unless the Tunisia project settings are documented and accepted

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Tunisia and China are both 50 Hz markets, but the exporter still needs Tunisia-specific STEG voltage and frequency settings, including the cited 47.5-52 Hz and -15% / +10% windows, plus commissioning evidence for the actual connection route.

source.publisher: Société Tunisienne de l'Electricité et du Gaz (STEG)

rows.requirement: ANME PV Program Lists and Eligible Installers
rows.target_requirement: ANME publishes PV program resources including accepted PV panel lists and eligible installer lists for grid-connected low-voltage and medium-voltage PV installations. These lists are not the same as a universal inverter type approval, but they show that Tunisia project eligibility can depend on current ANME program documents, eligible service providers, and project-file requirements.
target_standards: ANME PV downloads and current program lists
ANME eligible installers for grid-connected BT PV installations
ANME eligible installers for grid-connected MT PV installations
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese supplier qualification lists, bankability files, and domestic installer approvals do not replace ANME or Tunisia project eligibility checks. They can be supporting evidence for supplier due diligence.
china_standards: Chinese installer or EPC qualification documents
rows.gap: Confirm whether the Tunisia buyer or program requires a named eligible installer, accepted product list, French warranty terms, local after-sales support, or ANME project documentation. Do not infer inverter eligibility from panel-list status.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory only where the project uses an ANME program, subsidy, eligibility list, tender, or buyer requirement that makes the listed-document route binding
mandatory_status.cn: Chinese domestic supplier or installer listings are not mandatory for Tunisia and do not replace ANME program eligibility

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] ANME list status is project-context evidence, not a blanket inverter approval. Check the current program documents before quoting eligibility to a Tunisian buyer.

source.publisher: Agence Nationale pour la Maîtrise de l'Energie (ANME)

rows.requirement: China GB/T Inverter Evidence Versus Tunisia Acceptance
rows.target_requirement: Tunisia-facing evidence should be organized around the Tunisian project route, IEC / NT standards, STEG connection review, and ANME program requirements. China GB/T standards can be useful technical evidence, but they do not by themselves prove Tunisian conformity unless the reviewer accepts them or a clause-by-clause equivalence file is approved.
target_standards: IEC 62109-1 / IEC 62109-2 where required
Applicable INNORPI NT standards
STEG / ANME project procedures
rows.china_equivalent: China evidence commonly includes GB/T 37408 for grid-connected photovoltaic inverters, GB/T 19964 for photovoltaic power-station grid connection, NB/T 32004-family technical specifications, EMC reports, and factory declarations. These documents should be treated as input evidence, not automatic Tunisia approvals.
rows.gap: Create a matrix that maps each GB/T or NB/T test item to the requested IEC, NT, STEG, ANME, tender, or contract clause. Mark unsupported clauses clearly and close them with additional IEC reports, lab statements, firmware setting records, or project tests.

mandatory_status.target: Tunisian acceptance is mandatory where required by the authority, utility, tender, buyer, financier, insurer, or project owner; China GB/T evidence is only supporting evidence unless expressly accepted
mandatory_status.cn: GB/T standards are common China technical references; their mandatory or voluntary status depends on the Chinese scheme, product, tender, and buyer requirement

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Use China GB/T reports as mapped technical evidence, not as a substitute for Tunisia project approval, IEC / NT evidence, or STEG / ANME review.

source.publisher: National Public Service Platform for Standards Information (SAMR / SAC)

rows.requirement: STEG / ANME Grid-Connection Review for PV Self-Production
rows.target_requirement: Grid-connected PV projects in Tunisia must follow the applicable STEG connection and tariff procedures and ANME program or eligibility route. For low-voltage (BT) PV self-production, the cited Tunisia route uses a 200 kWc capacity limit and a 40 kWc threshold above which a STEG technical study is required. STEG also publishes renewable-energy autoproductor procedures, and ANME publishes PV resources and lists for grid-connected BT and MT installations.
target_standards: STEG renewable-energy autoproductor tariff and procedure pages
Tunisia BT PV self-production route: 200 kWc capacity limit and STEG technical study above 40 kWc
STEG simplified procedure for renewable-energy autoproducteurs connected to the medium-voltage network
ANME PV resources for grid-connected BT and MT installations
rows.china_equivalent: China grid-connection reports and factory settings are usually based on Chinese grid codes and buyer specifications. They should be reconfigured and documented for Tunisia's STEG connection study, protection settings, anti-islanding behavior, voltage and frequency windows, and commissioning tests.
rows.gap: Prepare a Tunisia-specific grid file: single-line diagram, inverter certificates, protection relay settings, anti-islanding evidence, power-quality data, commissioning protocol, French technical documents where requested, and a settings lock or change-control record for the deployed firmware.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for any project that connects to the STEG network or seeks ANME / STEG program, tariff, self-production, surplus-sale, or connection approval
mandatory_status.cn: Chinese grid-code compliance is useful evidence but does not replace STEG / ANME connection review or Tunisia-specific settings

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] A China grid certificate is not a Tunisia grid-connection approval. For BT PV, check the 200 kWc capacity limit and the 40 kWc STEG study threshold, and treat STEG / ANME review, project settings, and commissioning evidence as separate deliverables.

source.publisher: Société Tunisienne de l'Electricité et du Gaz (STEG)

rows.requirement: INNORPI NT Standards and Project-Specified Standards
rows.target_requirement: INNORPI is Tunisia's national standards body and may adopt Tunisian NT standards based on international standards. For solar PV inverters, exporters should verify whether the tender, customs route, conformity route, STEG connection file, or project owner cites VDE 0126-1-1 certification, NT 67.74 for EMC, NT 87.47-7-712 for PV electrical installations, IEC 62109, or other PV, EMC, low-voltage, or grid standards. A standard may be voluntary in general but mandatory once referenced by regulation, procurement, connection approval, or contract.
target_standards: Applicable Norme Tunisienne (NT) standards published or adopted by INNORPI
STEG-cited VDE 0126-1-1 inverter certificate where required
NT 67.74 for electromagnetic compatibility where required
NT 87.47-7-712 for photovoltaic electrical installations where required
IEC 62109-1 / IEC 62109-2 where adopted, cited, or contractually required
Project-specific tender or procurement specifications
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese exporters often provide GB/T or NB/T reports, CQC files, and IEC CB reports. The Tunisia file should identify which documents are direct IEC evidence and which are China-specific evidence requiring equivalence review.
china_standards: NB/T 32004 series where used by buyer or project
IEC CB test report and certificate where available
rows.gap: Before shipment, request the buyer's exact NT / IEC list and check whether INNORPI, customs, utility, financing, or project procurement requires original certificates, French documentation, Arabic / French labels, or local conformity steps.

mandatory_status.target: Standards are not automatically mandatory solely because they exist; they become mandatory when incorporated into Tunisian regulation, customs or conformity controls, grid-connection approval, tender, financing, insurance, or contract requirements
mandatory_status.cn: China GB/T or NB/T evidence is not automatically accepted in Tunisia unless the Tunisian authority, utility, tender, or buyer recognizes it or accepts a mapped equivalence file

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat INNORPI / NT applicability as a scope question, not a guess. The export file should name each VDE, NT, or IEC item required by the Tunisian project, including VDE 0126-1-1, NT 67.74, and NT 87.47-7-712 where cited, and show how China evidence maps to it.

source.publisher: Institut National de la Normalisation et de la Propriété Industrielle (INNORPI)

rows.target_requirement: Tunisia's renewable-electricity framework includes Law 2015-12 on electricity production from renewable energies. PV self-production projects should confirm the applicable regime, authorization or declaration route, surplus-sale treatment, STEG contract or tariff handling, ANME involvement, and whether the inverter documentation is part of the technical file for the project rather than a standalone market-access approval.
target_standards: Tunisia Law 2015-12 on electricity production from renewable energies
STEG tariffs for renewable-energy surplus purchase
ANME renewable-energy and PV program documentation
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese domestic distributed-PV filing, grid-connection, and subsidy documents do not establish the Tunisian legal route. They may support project references but should not be presented as proof of Tunisian self-production authorization.
china_standards: Chinese distributed PV filing and grid-connection documents
rows.gap: For a Tunisia project, confirm whether the legal route is self-consumption, self-production with surplus sale, authorization, concession, or another project category. The inverter supplier should provide technical documents in a format the developer can attach to the STEG / ANME / authority file.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where the project falls under Tunisian renewable-electricity self-production, surplus-sale, authorization, concession, tariff, or STEG contract procedures
mandatory_status.cn: Chinese administrative approvals are not mandatory for Tunisia and do not replace Tunisian renewable-energy legal procedures

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Law 2015-12 is a project-route issue. The inverter file should support the Tunisian self-production or surplus-sale application, not imply automatic legal authorization.

source.publisher: Société Tunisienne de l'Electricité et du Gaz (STEG)

rows.requirement: PV Inverter Electrical Safety: IEC 62109 Evidence
rows.target_requirement: Tunisia projects and tenders commonly need inverter safety evidence aligned with international PV inverter standards. IEC 62109-1 covers general safety requirements for PV power conversion equipment, and IEC 62109-2 covers particular requirements for inverters. Whether the evidence is legally mandatory depends on the import route, INNORPI-adopted NT standard scope, tender documents, utility review, financing conditions, and project owner requirements.
target_standards: Applicable INNORPI-adopted NT standards where specified by project or authority
rows.china_equivalent: China suppliers may hold IEC 62109 CB reports, GB/T inverter reports, or manufacturer declarations. These can support the Tunisia file, but Chinese domestic reports should be cross-mapped to the exact IEC or NT clauses requested by the buyer, utility, or tender.
china_standards: IEC 62109 CB Scheme test report where available
rows.gap: Do not assume a China GB/T test report is accepted as-is. Prepare IEC 62109-1 and IEC 62109-2 certificates or test reports, model-family coverage, critical component lists, installation manual, nameplate ratings, and a clause mapping for any China-only evidence.

mandatory_status.target: Voluntary as a standalone international standard unless made mandatory by Tunisian regulation, INNORPI-adopted NT scope, import control, utility procedure, tender, financing, insurer, or project-owner documents
mandatory_status.cn: Commonly voluntary or project-required for export files unless a Chinese regulation, certification scheme, tender, or buyer specification makes it mandatory

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] IEC 62109 evidence is the safest baseline for Tunisia-facing inverter files, but it should be tied to the specific Tunisian project, tender, INNORPI / NT reference, and STEG / ANME review route.
